AmdLogEntry.description: The AMD OEM extension for a Redfish LogEntry / Message resource. Consolidates AMD-specific identifiers, serviceable units, rack location, and CPER-style error data.
AmdLogEntry.longDescription: This object shall represent the AMD OEM-specific properties that may appear inside the `Oem/AMD` section of a Redfish LogEntry, Message, or CPER record. It unifies the schemas observed across host/BMC log entries, firmware update verification failures, AMD CPU/EAM error reports, and AMD CPER OEM payloads.

^([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)?@(odata|Redfis.description: This property shall specify a valid odata or Redfish property.

AMDFieldIdentifiers.description: Array of AMD-specific field identifiers (AFID) describing the event and the affected serviceable units.
AMDFieldIdentifiers.longDescription: This property shall contain an array of AMD Field Identifier (AFID) entries that describe the event. Each entry includes a numeric AFID, a human-readable description, and the list of serviceable units associated with the event.

Description.description: Free-form description of the AMD OEM event.
Description.longDescription: This property shall contain a human-readable description of the AMD-specific event when no AFID entry is provided (for example, signal-level failures reported directly by the platform).

RackUnitPosition.description: Rack unit position (e.g. `U22`) of the affected chassis.
RackUnitPosition.longDescription: This property shall indicate the rack unit (RU) position of the chassis that produced the event, using the standard `U<n>` notation.

ErrDataArr.description: Array of AMD CPER OEM error-data records associated with the event.
ErrDataArr.longDescription: This property shall contain an array of AMD CPER (Common Platform Error Record) OEM payload entries. It is typically present when the parent record is a CPER notification rather than a standard LogEntry.

AMDFieldIdentifier.description: One AMD Field Identifier (AFID) entry describing a serviceable event.
AMDFieldIdentifier.longDescription: This object shall represent a single AFID entry. The AFID uniquely identifies a class of AMD-defined service event and links to the resources that must be serviced.

AFID.description: AMD Field Identifier numeric code (or placeholder string).
AFID.longDescription: This property shall contain the AMD-assigned Field Identifier (AFID) value. The canonical type is integer; a string value is permitted only when the AFID is not yet resolved (for example, a placeholder such as `<AFID Data>`).

Description.description: Human-readable description associated with the AFID.

ServiceableUnits.description: Array of references to Redfish resources that are serviceable for this AFID.
ServiceableUnits.longDescription: This property shall contain an array of links (`@odata.id`) to the Redfish resources (Processor, Chassis, TrustedComponent, etc.) that are the serviceable units for this AFID event.

ErrData.description: A single AMD CPER OEM error-data record.
ErrData.longDescription: This object shall describe one AMD CPER OEM error payload, including its metadata, source platform, and event report details.

ErrDataMetadata.description: Metadata classifying the AMD CPER error record.

context_type.description: Type of context that produced the record (e.g. `Crashdump`).

ErrDataPlatform.description: Platform-level identifiers for the AMD CPER error record.

apic_id.description: APIC ID of the logical processor that produced the event, expressed as a hexadecimal string.

ErrDataEventReport.description: Event-level details of the AMD CPER error record.

enumDescriptions.Informational: Informational event, no service action required.
enumDescriptions.Corrected: The error was detected and corrected by hardware/firmware.
enumDescriptions.NonFatal: Non-fatal error; system continues to operate.
